mirages.claim: ModelDownloadStatus at Model.kt:353-360 with constructor (status, receivedBytes, totalBytes, errorMessage)
mirages.reality: Lines 353-360 are the enum ModelDownloadStatusType. The data class ModelDownloadStatus is at lines 362-369 with constructor order (status, totalBytes, receivedBytes, errorMessage, bytesPerSecond, remainingMs). Constructor field order in tech-spec is swapped (receivedBytes vs totalBytes) and line range refers to the enum, not the data class.

mirages.location_in_tech_spec: Architecture / How it works (Load / inference flow point 3, line ~77)
mirages.reality: Actual signature is runInference(model, input: String, resultListener, cleanUpListener, onError, images, audioClips, coroutineScope, extraContext). input is a plain String. Contents.of(...) is built internally from input+images+audio inside runInference (line 280). Passing Contents.of(...) as input would not compile.

mirages.claim: resultListener(partial, done, error) — third arg is error
mirages.reality: ResultListener typealias in LlmModelHelper.kt:26-27 is (partialResult: String, done: Boolean, partialThinkingResult: String?) -> Unit. Third argument is partialThinkingResult (from message.channels["thought"]), not error. Errors go through a separate onError callback inside MessageCallback.

mirages.claim: resetConversation API 'закрывает conversation, пересоздаёт на том же engine' mutates model.instance
mirages.location_in_tech_spec: Architecture / How it works (point 5); User-Spec Deviations D6 ('Model.instance мутируется в LlmChatModelHelper.initialize/cleanUp/resetConversation')
mirages.reality: resetConversation (lines 152-207) calls instance.conversation.close() and then instance.conversation = newConversation (line 201). It mutates the conversation field of the existing LlmModelInstance object, NOT model.instance itself (only initialize and cleanUp mutate model.instance). Mostly semantic inaccuracy but the stale-instance guard claim based on this is slightly imprecise.

mirages.claim: kotlin-reflect is required for Gallery Config.kt to parse configValues via reflection
mirages.location_in_tech_spec: Risks T-R2 (line ~319); Dependencies (line 230, 'kotlin-reflect ... требуется для Model.configValues reflection-driven parse')
mirages.reality: Grep for kotlin.reflect, KClass, memberProperties, createInstance across entire gallery-source/Android/src returns ZERO matches. Config.kt does NOT use kotlin.reflect at all. configValues is a plain Map<String, Any>, no reflection is used for parsing. kotlin-reflect is declared in Gallery libs.versions.toml but the stated rationale in T-R2 is factually wrong — it may be a transitive requirement for something else (e.g., Hilt/KSP tooling) but not for Config.kt parsing.

The method in LlmChatViewModel.kt is named stopResponse (line 238), not stop. It calls model.runtimeHelper.stopResponse(model), which is a Model extension that forwards to LlmChatModelHelper.stopResponse, which does call instance.conversation.cancelProcess(). Chain is correct but direct method name is wrong.

mirages.claim: lifecycle-process is already in libs.versions.toml via lifecycle-runtime-ktx BOM
mirages.reality: lifecycle-process in Gallery libs.versions.toml is a separate library entry (line 67: androidx-lifecycle-process) with its own version ref lifecycleProcess=2.8.7, not through a BOM. Lifecycle libraries don't share a Jetpack BOM. The version happens to match lifecycleRuntimeKtx=2.8.7 by coincidence, not BOM alignment. Practical result (2.8.7 pinning) is correct, rationale is inaccurate.

mirages.claim: KSP plugin version '2.2.0-2.0.2' (или актуальный совместимый с Kotlin 2.2.0)
mirages.reality: Tech-spec states both 'ksp = 2.3.6' (Decision T2, line ~109, matching architecture.md) AND 'com.google.devtools.ksp:2.2.0-2.0.2' (line ~239, Gradle plugins). These are contradictory version strings within the same tech-spec. Gallery libs.versions.toml uses ksp=2.3.6 (decoupled KSP2 versioning). 2.2.0-2.0.2 is not the version Gallery uses and is not what Decision T2 commits to. Self-contradiction.

mirages.claim: DownloadWorker.kt:134-137 contains HF-token Authorization header code
mirages.location_in_tech_spec: Architecture / What we're building (line 31); Task 3 description (line 384)
mirages.reality: Actual token-handling block spans lines 134-136 (accessToken check + Log.d + connection.setRequestProperty("Authorization", ...)). Line 137 is the closing brace. Minor off-by-one in range specification. Patch instruction still correct in intent.

mirages.claim: inline const val TASK_ID_LLM_CHAT = "llm_chat" from Tasks.kt
mirages.reality: Could not verify the exact constant name TASK_ID_LLM_CHAT in Gallery Tasks.kt — file exists at gallery-source/.../data/Tasks.kt. Task ID constant may have a different name. Not a confirmed mirage, but the exact name should be verified during Task 3 implementation.

DownloadRepository.kt lines 123-125 contain if(model.accessToken != null) inputDataBuilder.putString(KEY_MODEL_DOWNLOAD_ACCESS_TOKEN, ...)
DownloadRepository.kt line 39 imports AppLifecycleProvider; line 83 has ctor param lifecycleProvider
DownloadRepository.kt line 276 contains 'if (lifecycleProvider.isAppInForeground) return' inside sendNotification

LiteRT-LM API: Engine, EngineConfig, Conversation, ConversationConfig, Contents.of, MessageCallback, Engine.createConversation, Conversation.cancelProcess, SamplerConfig, Backend.GPU/CPU/NPU all exist in litertlm 0.10.0 (imports at lines 34-46 of LlmChatModelHelper.kt resolve)
GalleryLifecycleProvider.kt and AppLifecycleProvider interface exist (extraction set claim)
Utils.kt contains cleanUpMediapipeTaskErrorMessage (line 57) and isPixel10 (line 347)
ModelAllowlist.kt contains AllowedModel data class and toModel() method (lines 45, 69) with Gson @SerializedName annotations
1_0_11.json contains Gemma-4-E2B-it and Gemma-4-E4B-it entries with modelId prefix litert-community/

Dependency versions confirmed in Gallery libs.versions.toml: litertlm=0.10.0, kotlin=2.2.0, ksp=2.3.6, workRuntime=2.10.0, gson=2.12.1, coreKtx=1.15.0, lifecycleRuntimeKtx=2.8.7, activityCompose=1.10.1, navigation=2.8.9, materialIconExtended=1.7.8, hiltNavigation=1.3.0, kotlinReflect=2.2.21, serializationJson=1.7.3, agp=8.8.2
architecture.md indeed pins compose-bom=2026.03.00 and hilt=2.57.1 (divergence from Gallery's 2026.02.00 and 2.57.2 is intentional per Decision T2)
Code-research sections §10.3, §10.4, §11.4, §12.3, §13, §14 exist and contain the referenced topics
ModelDownloadStatusType enum has 6 values: NOT_DOWNLOADED, PARTIALLY_DOWNLOADED, IN_PROGRESS, UNZIPPING, SUCCEEDED, FAILED
LlmChatModelHelper.stopResponse(model) already exists (lines 238-241), contrary to tech-spec T10 hedge 'если его нет'
Gallery uses KSP plugin (libs.versions.toml plugins block line 106), supports Decision T3 (Hilt via KSP is feasible)
